33	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the

94	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
95	
96	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
97	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
98	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
99	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
100	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
101	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
102	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
103	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
104	
105	namespace PresheafOfModules
106	
107	universe v'
108	
109	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
110	
111	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
112	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
113	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
114	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
115	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
116	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
117	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
118	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
119	  naturality {X Y} f := by
120	    ext r
121	    dsimp
122	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
123	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
124	    simp only [map_one]
125	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
126	
127	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
128	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
129	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
130	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
131	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
132	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
133	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
134	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
135	  app X := by
136	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
137	  naturality {X Y} f := by
138	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
139	    dsimp
140	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
141	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
142	    rfl
143	
144	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
145	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
146	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
147	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
148	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
149	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
150	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
151	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
152	  μ_natural_left := by
153	    intro X Y f X'
154	    ext1 Z
155	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
156	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
157	  μ_natural_right := by
158	    intro X Y X' f
159	    ext1 Z
160	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
161	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
162	  associativity := by
163	    intro M N P
164	    ext1 Z
165	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
166	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
167	  left_unitality := by
168	    intro M
169	    ext1 Z
170	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
171	      (M.obj Z)
172	  right_unitality := by
173	    intro M
174	    ext1 Z
175	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
176	      (M.obj Z)
177	
178	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
179	sheafification localizer
180	
181	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
182	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
183	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
184	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
185	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
186	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
187	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
188	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
189	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
190	structure. -/
191	
192	section FlatWhisker
193	
194	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
195	
196	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
197	
198	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
199	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
200	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
201	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
202	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
203	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
204	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
205	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
206	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
207	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
208	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
209	
210	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
211	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
212	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
213	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
214	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
215	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
216	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
217	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
218	
219	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
220	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
221	half. -/
222	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
223	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
224	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
225	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
226	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
227	  constructor
228	  intro U s
229	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
230	  | zero =>
231	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
232	      intro V i _
233	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
234	  | tmul a b =>
235	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
236	      intro V i hi
237	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
238	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
239	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
240	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
241	      congr 1
242	  | add s t hs ht =>
243	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
244	      intro V i hi
245	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
246	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
247	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
248	
249	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
250	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
251	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
252	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
253	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
254	injectivity half. -/
255	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
256	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
257	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
258	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
259	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
260	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
261	  constructor
262	  intro X ξ η h
263	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
264	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
265	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
266	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
267	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
268	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
269	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
270	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
271	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
272	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
273	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
274	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
275	  have hex : Function.Exact
276	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
277	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
278	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
279	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
280	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
281	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
282	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
283	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
284	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
285	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
286	    intro ζ
287	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
288	    | zero =>
289	        rw [map_zero]
290	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
291	    | tmul a kk =>
292	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
293	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
294	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
295	          rw [map_zero]
296	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
297	          exact kk.2
298	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
299	        intro V f hf
300	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
301	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
302	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
303	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
304	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
305	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
306	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
307	        rw [map_add]
308	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
309	        intro V f hf
310	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
311	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
312	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
313	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
314	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
315	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
316	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
317	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
318	  intro V f hf
319	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
320	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
321	  exact hf
322	
323	/-- **Flat left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.**
324	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
325	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
326	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
327	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
328	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
329	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).

333	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
334	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
335	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)

337	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
338	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢

342	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
343	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
344	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
345	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
346	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
347	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
348	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
349	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
350	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
351	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
352	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
353	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
354	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
355	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
356	  have hconj : g ▷ F
357	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
358	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
359	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
360	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
361	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
362	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
363	  exact hwl

502	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
503	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
504	
505	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
506	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
507	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
508	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
509	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
510	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
511	
512	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
513	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
514	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
515	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
516	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
517	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
518	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
519	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
520	
521	section StalkLinearMap
522	
523	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
524	
525	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
526	
527	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
528	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
529	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
530	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
531	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
532	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
533	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
534	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
535	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
536	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
537	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
538	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
539	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
540	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
541	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
542	  map_smul' r s := by
543	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
544	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
545	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
546	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
547	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
548	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
549	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
550	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
551	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
552	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
553	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
554	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
555	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
556	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
557	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
558	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
559	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
560	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
561	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
562	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
563	      intro z
564	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
565	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
566	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
567	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
568	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
569	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
570	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
571	
572	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
573	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
574	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
575	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
576	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
577	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
578	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
579	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
580	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
581	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
582	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
583	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
584	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
585	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
586	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
587	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
588	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
589	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
590	
591	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
592	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
593	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
594	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
595	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
596	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
597	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
598	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
599	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
600	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
601	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
602	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
603	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
604	
605	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
606	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
607	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
608	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
609	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
610	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
611	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
612	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
613	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
614	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
615	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
616	
617	end StalkLinearMap

629	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
630	
631	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
632	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
633	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
634	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
635	
636	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
637	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body lifts
638	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
639	the small Zariski site of `X`. -/
640	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
641	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
642	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
643	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
644	
645	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
646	
647	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
648	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
649	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
650	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
651	
652	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. iter-202 Lane TS
653	scaffold: the body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body inherits the

1	\chapter{Relative Picard sheaf --- \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} substrate (A.1.c.SubT)}
2	\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}
3	
4	\section{Motivation: the abelian-group-valued relative Picard sheaf}
5	\label{sec:tensorobj_motivation}
6	
7	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
8	\(k\). In Kleiman's framework (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor},
9	\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}), the relative Picard functor
10	\[
11	  \Pic_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
12	  \qquad
13	  T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^* \Pic(T),
14	\]
15	is, by definition, abelian-group-valued. The abelian-group structure on each
16	fiber \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) comes from tensor product of line bundles
17	(Stacks tag 01CR / Hartshorne~II.\S 6), and the structure descends to the
18	quotient because \(\pi_T^*\) is a group homomorphism.
19	
20	% SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
21	% Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
22	% abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
23	% groups)
24	% (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
25	% SOURCE QUOTE:
26	% "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
27	%  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
28	%  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
29	% abelian groups defined by the formula
30	% 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
31	% \end{dfn}
32	%  [\dots]
33	% \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
34	%   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
35	% 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
36	%  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
37	% topologies  by
38	% 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
39	% 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
40	%  \end{dfn}"
41	\textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.
42	The absolute and relative Picard functors take values in the category of
43	abelian groups; the relative functor is the quotient of one such group by
44	another, and the structure morphisms of the resulting presheaf are group
45	homomorphisms.}
46	
47	Concretely, the abelian-group law on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the assignment
48	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr] :=
49	\bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
50	with neutral element the class of the structure sheaf
51	\(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) and inverse
52	\(- \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\), where
53	\(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}}(\mathcal{L},
54	\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})\) is the dual invertible sheaf. Pullback of
55	\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) preserves tensor
56	products and the structure sheaf, so the image
57	\(\pi_T^* \Pic(T) \subset \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a subgroup, and the quotient
58	\(\Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\) inherits a canonical abelian-group
59	structure. This is the content of
60	\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
61	
62	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
63	infrastructural, and --- crucially --- much smaller than a full
64	monoidal-category construction. The group law lives on \emph{isomorphism
65	classes} of line bundles: each group axiom is a \emph{proposition} asserting
66	the existence of an isomorphism (\(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\)),
67	and a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it. In
68	particular the coherence data of a monoidal category --- the pentagon,
69	triangle and hexagon identities, and a fortiori any closed structure --- is
70	\emph{never consumed} by a group law on iso-classes. What the law genuinely
71	requires on the carrier is therefore only:
72	\begin{enumerate}
73	  \item a binary tensor-product operation
74	    \(\otimes\, : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
75	                  \to \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), well-defined on
76	    isomorphism classes;
77	  \item the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as a designated unit object
78	    for \(\otimes\);
79	  \item the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
80	    (\(\exists N,\ M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)) together with the three
81	    monoidal coherence isomorphisms of \(\otimes_X\): associativity
82	    \((M \otimes N) \otimes P \cong M \otimes (N \otimes P)\), unit
83	    \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \cong M\) (and on the right), and commutativity
84	    \(M \otimes N \cong N \otimes M\). The inverse is carried by the
85	    predicate, not by a separate lemma.
86	\end{enumerate}
87	At Mathlib's pinned commit, the entire monoidal scaffolding for (3) is already
88	available abstractly, and the project's task is to \emph{instantiate} it rather
89	than to hand-assemble the coherence data. Two facts drive this. First, the
90	category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves of modules over a
91	presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) is already a (symmetric) monoidal category in
92	Mathlib (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\)), with the sectionwise
93	relative tensor \((M \otimes_R N)(U) = M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)\); this applies
94	verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf \(R = \mathcal{O}_X\). Second,
95	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
96	(\(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)) takes a
97	monoidal category \(C\), a localization functor \(L : C \to D\) at a morphism
98	property \(W\), and an instance \(W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), and produces a
99	\emph{full} monoidal-category structure on the localization \(D\) --- associator,
100	left/right unitors, braiding, and \emph{all} coherence (pentagon, triangle,
101	naturalities) derived. Sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the
102	localization \(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class
103	\(W = J.W\), so the monoidal structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) is
104	\emph{free} from this API once \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is supplied.
105	
106	Consequently the only genuinely-new obligation is the single instance
107	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) for the sheafification localizer on
108	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\). Its two fields are the
109	left/right whiskering-stability statements \(J.W\,g \Rightarrow J.W(F \triangleleft
110	g)\) and \(J.W\,g \Rightarrow J.W(g \triangleright F)\) for an \emph{arbitrary}
111	module \(F\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}), and these are flatness-free: a
112	\(J.W\)-morphism is a stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x =
113	\mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) is again an isomorphism (tensoring with an
114	isomorphism preserves isomorphisms --- no flatness, no local triviality). The
115	load-bearing half is the local-injectivity statement
116	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, whose stalkwise proof needs two
117	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\)-level ingredients that are Mathlib-absent at the
118	pinned commit (the module-level stalk characterisation of \(J.W\), and the
119	commutation of the stalk with the relative tensor); this is the
120	\emph{sole remaining open obligation} for the whole group-law engine.
121	
122	With \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) in hand, the substrate \(\otimes_X\) carries the
123	associator, unitors and braiding from \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\), and the
124	group law assembles the commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes whose units
125	group is the Picard group (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), exactly as
126	Mathlib builds \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) as
127	\(\mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\). The
128	\(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible},
129	Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) idiom) carries each inverse
130	existentially. The geometric compatibility isomorphism
131	\(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) and any
132	separate tensor-inverse lemma are \emph{off} this critical path; see also
133	\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
134

228	\paragraph{The gap and route~(e).} The substrate

265	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
266	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
267	
268	\begin{definition}
269	
270	\leanok
271	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
272	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
274	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
275	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
276	  \[
277	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
278	  \]
279	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
280	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
281	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
282	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
283	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
284	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
285	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
286	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
287	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
288	  canonical compatibility
289	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
290	     \;\cong\;
291	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
292	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
293	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
294	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
295	
296	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
297	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
298	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
299	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
300	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
301	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
302	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
303	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
304	\end{definition}
305	
306	\begin{lemma}
307	
308	\leanok
309	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
310	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
311	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
312	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
313	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
314	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
315	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
316	  \[
317	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
318	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
319	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
320	  \]
321	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
322	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
323	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
324	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
325	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
326	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
327	  \[
328	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
329	    \qquad
330	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
331	  \]
332	  the associator
333	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
334	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
335	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
336	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
337	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
338	\end{lemma}
339	
340	\begin{proof}
341	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
342	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
343	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
344	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
345	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
346	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
347	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
348	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
349	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
350	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
351	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path

353	\end{proof}

355	\begin{remark}
356	[The full monoidal category is cheap via route~(e); the group law consumes only propositions]
357	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
358	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
359	        lem:whisker_of_W}
360	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
361	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
362	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
363	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
364	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
365	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. Under route~(e) this is
366	  \emph{cheap}, not expensive: it is exactly the output of
367	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
368	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
369	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), once the single instance
370	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}) is supplied. All the
371	  coherence is then derived by the API.
372	
373	  Crucially, \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) needs \emph{no} closed structure. Its
374	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
375	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
376	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}: a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
377	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
378	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\) (tensoring with an isomorphism
379	  preserves isomorphisms). This is the same technique Mathlib uses for
380	  fixed-base presheaves in
381	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\), ported to
382	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). It uses neither
383	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
384	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
385	  false over a non-affine open and is therefore off the critical path); the
386	  earlier reading that arbitrary-\(F\) whisker stability requires
387	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) was the \emph{wrong altitude}.
388	
389	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
390	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
391	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
392	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
393	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
394	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
395	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
396	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
397	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
398	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, all now supplied by the route~(e) monoidal structure;
399	  none of them is built from the off-path restriction-compatibility isomorphism
400	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. This mirrors Mathlib's own Picard group
401	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}),
402	  whose \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is transported from
403	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\): there
404	  over a fixed ring, here over the sheafification localizer via
405	  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\).
406	\end{remark}

412	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
413	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
414	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
415	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the

421	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and the interaction with the pullback
422	functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism

426	\begin{lemma}
427	
428	\leanok
429	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
430	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
431	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
432	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
433	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
434	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
435	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
436	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
437	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
438	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
439	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
440	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
441	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
442	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
443	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
444	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
445	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
446	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
447	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
448	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
449	  \[
450	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
451	      \;\;:=\;\;
452	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
453	  \]
454	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
455	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
456	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
457	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
458	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
459	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
460	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
461	\end{lemma}
462	
463	\begin{proof}
464	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
465	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
466	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
467	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
468	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
469	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
470	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
471	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
472	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
473	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
474	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
475	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
476	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
477	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
478	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
479	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
480	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
481	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
482	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
483	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
484	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).

486	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
487	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
488	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
489	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
490	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
491	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
492	\end{proof}

494	\begin{lemma}
495	
496	\leanok
497	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
498	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
499	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
500	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
501	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
502	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
503	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
504	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
505	  morphism
506	  \[
507	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
508	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
509	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
510	  \]
511	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
512	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
513	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
514	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
515	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
516	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
517	\end{lemma}
518	
519	\begin{proof}
520	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
521	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
522	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
523	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
524	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
525	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
526	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
527	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
528	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
529	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
530	  isomorphism}
531	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
532	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
533	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
534	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
535	  therefore extension of scalars along
536	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
537	
538	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
539	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
540	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
541	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
542	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
543	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
544	  the abstract pullback functor.
545	
546	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
547	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
548	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
549	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
550	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
551	   \;\cong\;
552	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
553	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
554	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
555	  comparison
556	  \[
557	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
558	      \;\longrightarrow\;
559	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
560	      \;\otimes\;
561	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
562	  \]
563	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
564	
565	  \emph{Step 3 (off the critical path).} Completing this comparison at the
566	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise description of the abstract functor
567	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
568	  adjoint of pushforward and admits no sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the
569	  pinned commit. The genuine residual decomposes into two Mathlib-absent
570	  presheaf-level ingredients --- a presheaf-level identification
571	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) via
572	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) (needing a presheaf-level pushforward
573	  adjunction, only the sheaf version of which exists), and a strong-monoidal
574	  upgrade of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the ring isomorphism
575	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\). This lemma is therefore \emph{not} on the critical path
576	  for the relative Picard group law: the group law is built from
577	  \(\otimes\)-invertibility (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) and the
578	  coherence isomorphisms
579	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
580	  none of which consume restriction-compatibility. The statement is retained
581	  only as an optional supplement; its full proof is deferred.
582	\end{proof}

584	\begin{lemma}
585	
586	\leanok
587	[Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial]
588	  \label{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
589	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial}
590	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
591	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, and let
592	  \(L, L' \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be locally-trivial modules of
593	  rank one (i.e.\ line bundles). The tensor product \(L \otimes_X L'\) of
594	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is again locally trivial of rank

600	\begin{proof}
601	  Pick a common affine open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on which both
602	  \(L|_{U_i}\) and \(L'|_{U_i}\) are free of rank one. On each \(U_i\)
603	  there are trivialisations \(L|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and
604	  \(L'|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). The tensor product of two free
605	  rank-one modules over a commutative ring is again free of rank one;

611	\begin{lemma}

613	[Flat whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]
614	  \label{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}

616	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
617	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck
618	  topology \(J\), and let \(J.W\) be the class of morphisms of presheaves of
619	  \(R\)-modules that are inverted by sheafification --- equivalently, the
620	  \emph{locally bijective} morphisms, i.e.\ those that are both locally injective
621	  and locally surjective for \(J\) (the characterisation
622	  \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\)). Let \(F\) be a presheaf of \(R\)-modules
623	  that is \emph{flat} (sectionwise: \(F(U)\) is a flat \(R(U)\)-module for every
624	  object \(U\) of the site). Then for every morphism \(g\) in \(J.W\) the

626	  \[
627	    F \triangleleft g \;\;=\;\; \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g
628	  \]
629	  (the action of \(F \otimes^{p} -\) on \(g\), with \(\otimes^{p}\) the
630	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product) again lies in \(J.W\). By the symmetry of
631	  the presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, the right-whiskered morphism

634	  Both halves --- the left-whiskering statement and its braiding-conjugate
635	  right-whiskering statement --- are established. They are valid standalone
636	  results about flat whiskering of sheafification-localizer morphisms. They are,

646	  independent, closed result, superseded on the critical path by the
647	  \(\mathtt{\_of\_W}\) variants of \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}.

649	
650	\begin{proof}
651	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
652	  By the locally-bijective characterisation of the sheafification localizer
653	  (\(J.W\) is exactly the class of morphisms that are \(J\)-locally injective and
654	  \(J\)-locally surjective), it suffices to show that \(F \triangleleft g\) is
655	  both locally injective and locally surjective whenever \(g\) is.
656	
657	  The presheaf-of-modules tensor product is computed sectionwise,
658	  \((F \otimes^{p} G)(U) = F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} G(U)\), so for
659	  \(g : A \to B\) the whiskered morphism acts on sections over \(U\) as the
660	  \(R(U)\)-linear map
661	  \[
662	    \mathrm{id}_{F(U)} \otimes g(U) \;\colon\;
663	      F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} A(U) \;\longrightarrow\; F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} B(U).
664	  \]
665	
666	  \emph{Local surjectivity} is automatic and needs no hypothesis on \(F\):
667	  tensoring is right exact, so applying \(F \otimes^{p} -\) carries the cokernel
668	  of \(g\) to the cokernel of \(F \triangleleft g\); since \(g\) is locally
669	  surjective its cokernel is locally zero, hence so is the cokernel of
670	  \(F \triangleleft g\), and \(F \triangleleft g\) is locally surjective.
671	
672	  \emph{Local injectivity} is where flatness enters. Because each \(F(U)\) is a
673	  flat \(R(U)\)-module, the functor \(F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} -\) preserves injective
674	  linear maps (Mathlib
675	  \(\mathtt{Module.Flat.lTensor\_preserves\_injective\_linearMap}\)). Thus
676	  whenever \(g(U)\) is injective on a covering sieve, so is
677	  \((\mathrm{id}_{F(U)} \otimes g(U))\); the local injectivity of \(g\) is
678	  therefore transported sectionwise to \(F \triangleleft g\).
679	
680	  Locally injective and locally surjective together give
681	  \(F \triangleleft g \in J.W\). The right-whiskered statement
682	  \(g \triangleright F\) follows by conjugating \(F \triangleleft g\) with the
683	  braiding of the symmetric presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, which is an
684	  isomorphism and hence preserves membership in \(J.W\); the flatness of \(F\) is
685	  the only input, and it is symmetric in the two tensor factors.
686	\end{proof}

737	The monoidal structure on the substrate is realized by \emph{instantiating}
738	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API

741	presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) and the

743	instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), whose two fields are the
744	whiskering-stability lemmas below. Their load-bearing local-injectivity half is

780	  isomorphism}: at each point \(x\) the induced map on stalks \(g_x\) is an
781	  isomorphism. The presheaf-of-modules tensor is sectionwise, so it commutes with
782	  the stalk (a filtered colimit), giving

44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73

1356	\section{Consumer: the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance for the relative Picard sheaf}
1357	\label{sec:tensorobj_consumer_acg}
1358	
1359	The substrate of \cref{sec:tensorobj_substrate,sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
1360	supplies all the data missing from \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}'s Lean
1361	encoding. The consumer instance below packages the abelian-group law
1362	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1363	 := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\) on the
1364	relative Picard quotient via the substrate.
1365	
1366	\begin{theorem}
1367	
1368	\leanok
1369	[Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient via \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)]
1370	  \label{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}
1371	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj}
1372	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1373	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1374	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1375	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1376	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
1377	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
1378	        lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,
1379	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1380	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1381	
1382	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
1383	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
1384	  % abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
1385	  % groups)
1386	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
1387	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1388	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
1389	  %  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
1390	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
1391	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
1392	  % 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
1393	  % \end{dfn}
1394	  %  [\dots]
1395	  % \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
1396	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
1397	  % 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
1398	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
1399	  % topologies  by
1400	  % 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
1401	  % 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
1402	  %  \end{dfn}"
1403	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
1404	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
1405	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
1406	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
1407	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
1408	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
1409	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
1410	  \[
1411	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
1412	  \]
1413	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
1414	  abelian-group structure with addition
1415	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1416	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
1417	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
1418	  inverse
1419	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
1420	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
1421	  supplied by the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1422	  restricted to the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier
1423	  via \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
1424	  map
1425	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
1426	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
1427	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1428	\end{theorem}
1429	
1430	\begin{proof}
1431	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1432	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1433	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1434	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1435	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1436	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1437	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
1438	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
1439	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
1440	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
1441	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
1442	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
1443	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
1444	  symmetrically in the second argument. The abelian-group structure on
1445	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is then exactly the \emph{units group of the
1446	  commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes}
1447	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}): multiplication
1448	  \([L]\cdot[L'] = [L \otimes_{C\times_k T} L']\) is well-defined and
1449	  commutative-monoidal by the coherence isomorphisms
1450	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1451	  and an iso-class is a unit precisely when its carrier is
1452	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse
1453	  being carried by the predicate. Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the
1454	  existence of an isomorphism --- so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon,
1455	  triangle, hexagon) is invoked; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on
1456	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the additive form of this units group. This mirrors
1457	  Mathlib's own Picard group: \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its
1458	  \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\),
1459	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) transported from
1460	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\); here
1461	  the same \(\mathrm{Units}(\cdots)\) shape is transported to the scheme-level
1462	  \(\otimes_X\). (The scheme-level Picard group is genuinely absent from
1463	  Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup} records ``connect to
1464	  invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project supplies it lightly.)
1465	  The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1466	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the scheme-level analogue of
1467	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\); the geometric predicate
1468	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) is retained separately and is connected to
1469	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) only by an off-critical-path equivalence (only the
1470	  geometric \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)-style
1471	  direction is nontrivial, and it is not needed here).
1472	
1473	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
1474	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
1475	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
1476	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
1477	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
1478	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
1479	
1480	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
1481	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
1482	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
1483	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
1484	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
1485	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
1486	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
1487	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
1488	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
1489	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1490	\end{proof}
